summary: Exceptional audience recording of a high‑energy spring ’85 show, highlighted by a smoking China > Rider, soaring Bird Song and a rare Tom Thumb’s Blues duet.
review: The Oades‑brothers AUD captures the night with crystal‑clear ambience that makes you feel like you’re in the front row.  Musically the band is on fire – the second set rolls flawlessly from China > Rider into an inspired Estimated > Eyes, a blistering Other One and a beautiful Stella Blue, while Tom Thumb’s Blues features a unique vocal duet with Phil.  Minor slips (a rushed Estimated and a couple of missteps in Might As Well) don’t dent the overall brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: All 11 reviews are 4‑5 stars, praising both performance and sound; the show is repeatedly called a spring ’85 highlight and the AUD is described as exceptional.

recording_quality.technical_notes: AUD capture is crisp, immersive and balances crowd ambience with instrument detail; reviewers note no drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but uneventful; second set drives the show with seamless segues and high‑energy jams, delivering a satisfying arc that ends on a joyous Around > NFA.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice described as rugged or not at its best yet powerful; fluid guitar playing praised, solos called “extra good”, and he sings a duet with Phil on Tom Thumb’s Blues.
band_member_comments.Phil: Delivers the classic April Fools joke after Tom Thumb’s Blues, enjoys the spotlight during that song and joins Jerry in vocal duet.
band_member_comments.Bob: Passionate vocals highlighted on Estimated > Eyes, adding intensity to the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ tone called sweet and a key element of the show’s sound throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart get extra time after the set; Drums>Space segment noted as strong.
